item_name: AMOUNT OF NET LOCAL COUNTRY CLAIMS (INCLUDING DERIVATIVE PRODUCTS)

NOTE: Reported in Part A for the FFIEC 009a report.

NOTE: Reported in Part A for the FFIEC 009a report. NOTE: Reported in Part A for the FFIEC 009a report.

The term 'bank' covers the following institutions in foreign countries: commercial banks; savings banks; discount houses; and other similar institutions accepting short term deposits. It includes banking institutions owned by foreign governments unless such institutions function as central banks or banks of issue. (The latter are shown as 'public' borrowers.) item_name: DISTRIBUTION OF AMOUNT IN ITEM 5215 BY PUBLIC SECTOR ENTITIES

item_name: DISTRIBUTION OF AMOUNT IN ITEM 5215 BY OTHERS
description: Includes the distributed amount in item 5215 by others. 'All other borrowers' covers claims on persons; businesses; and institutions other than 'banks' and 'public borrowers' defined in items 5218 and 5219. NOTE: Reported in Part A for the FFIEC 009a report. COMPARABILITY: Prior to 3/31/97; included the distributed amount in item 5217.

item_name: DISTRIBUTION OF AMOUNT IN ITEM 5215 WITH TIME REMAINING TO MATURITY OF ONE YEAR AND UNDER

item_name: DISTRIBUTION OF AMOUNT IN ITEM 5215 WITH TIME REMAINING TO MATURITY OF OVER ONE YEAR
description: Includes the distributed amount in item 5215 with a time remaining to maturity of over one year. NOTE: Reported in Part A for the FFIEC 009a report. COMPARABILITY: Prior to 3/31/97; included the distributed amount in item 5217.

item_name: TOTAL AMOUNT OF EXPOSURE TO ALL OF THE COUNTRIES LISTED IN PART B
description: Includes the total amount of exposure to all of the countries listed in Part B. Provided is the name of each country not listed in Part A where the institution has exposure; as defined in item 4254 of Part A; that exceeds 0.75 percent but does not exceed 1 percent of the reporting institution's assets or is between 15 percent and 20 percent of its total capital; whichever is less. NOTE: Reported in Part B for the FFIEC 009a report.

item_name: LEGAL ENTITY IDENTIFIER
description: The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-digit alpha-numeric reference code to uniquely identify a legally distinct entity that engages in a financial transaction. The purpose for the LEI is to have a unified global identification system for legal entities across markets and jurisdictions. The LEI will be a linchpin for financial data-the first global and unique entity identifier enabling risk managers and regulators to identify parties to financial transactions instantly and precisely.
